summary: Executed S1-02 validation across source regressions, build/lint gates, and production UI integration. Source-level S1-02 behavior is implemented and regression tests pass, but live production network calls still fetch appointments without date_time gte/lte filters, indicating deployed bundle/runtime mismatch.

integration_issues.issue: On production https://cut-sync.vercel.app, Supabase /rest/v1/appointments requests were captured without date_time gte/lte parameters, even after period toggles and manual refresh; expected S1-02 interval-scoped queries were not observed.

action_items: Redeploy the latest source commit for AdminDashboardExperience/useAppointments and revalidate production network traces for /rest/v1/appointments include date_time=gte and date_time=lte for daily + period queries.
After redeploy, rerun live admin flow: period toggles + sync button and confirm two independent range-bound query streams are active.
Keep Node runtime in CI/deploy at 22.x (package engines) to avoid tooling inconsistencies seen in this container.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboardExperience.tsx is 850+ lines and exceeds maintainability threshold; split into focused modules/hooks (metrics, calendar, appointment actions, performance panel).
Daily and period mapping logic to RichAppointment is duplicated; extract shared mapper to reduce drift risk.
Source implementation correctly separates dailyRange (selectedDate) and periodRange (period), and period toggles do not mutate selectedDate in observed UI behavior.

success_rate.frontend: 65% (core admin UI reachable and period/date behavior visible, but production query integration mismatch persists)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Production still appears stale versus source. Use admin credentials, open dashboard, toggle Hoje/Semana/Mês, press Atualizar, and capture Supabase appointments requests to verify date_time gte/lte presence.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: login admin on production, open dashboard, toggle analytics period, inspect /rest/v1/appointments requests. Observed requests contained establishment_id + order only, no date_time gte/lte. Source code and local regressions confirm dateFrom/dateTo logic exists, so most likely RCA is deployment/runtime mismatch (older web bundle served) rather than current repository code.
test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and used admin account for production UI validation.
